---
title: "xG From Scratch & the Finishing-Skill Question"
subtitle: "Calibrated probabilistic prediction on StatsBomb Open Data"
author: "Alejandro Izaguirre"
date: today
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    code-fold: true
    df-print: kable
    embed-resources: true
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{r setup}
library(dplyr)
source(here::here("R", "utils.R"))
rd  <- function(f) readRDS(here::here("data", f))
fig <- function(f) file.path("..", "figures", f)   # relative to the .qmd, so it embeds
shots <- rd("shots_model.rds")
```

## Data & exclusions

StatsBomb Open Data (event + freeze-frame), pulled as raw JSON. Shots are the
unit of analysis. **Penalties are excluded** (a different process); direct free
kicks are kept but flagged. `shot_statsbomb_xg` is retained purely as a
benchmark and is **never** a feature.

```{r data-summary}
s <- shots   # alias so the `shots` column below doesn't shadow the data frame
tibble::tibble(
  shots = nrow(s),
  goals = sum(s$is_goal),
  base_rate = round(mean(s$is_goal), 3),
  players = dplyr::n_distinct(s$player_id),
  matches = dplyr::n_distinct(s$match_id),
  competitions = dplyr::n_distinct(s$competition)
)
```

Class balance by competition:

```{r balance}
rd("eda_balance.rds")
```

## EDA

The visual hook — goal rate over pitch location — plus the empirical goal-rate
curves against the two dominant geometric features.

![Goal rate by shot location](`r fig("shot_map.png")`)

![Goal rate vs distance](`r fig("goalrate_distance.png")`){width=70%}
![Goal rate vs angle](`r fig("goalrate_angle.png")`){width=70%}

## Models & leakage discipline

Three models on **identical match-grouped CV folds** (shots from one match never
span train/test), with out-of-fold predictions for every shot:

- **A — logistic**: `distance + angle + body part` (interpretable baseline).
- **B — GAM**: `mgcv` 2-D spatial smooth `s(x, y)` + context terms.
- **C — xgboost**: full feature set, `nrounds` chosen by inner CV.

```{r leaderboard}
rd("model_leaderboard.rds")
```

## Calibration & benchmark vs StatsBomb

Great ranking is not enough — predicted probabilities must be **calibrated**.
Reliability diagram (out-of-fold), with ECE and the Murphy/Brier decomposition.

![Reliability diagram (out-of-fold)](`r fig("reliability.png")`){width=70%}

```{r calib}
rd("calibration_scores.rds")
```

If the GBM is miscalibrated, leakage-safe isotonic recalibration (fit on other
folds, applied out-of-fold):

```{r recal}
rd("recalibration.rds")
```

**Framing:** the question is "how close can open features get to a production
model," not "I beat StatsBomb." The gap to `statsbomb_xg` is reported honestly
above.

## Finishing skill: real or regression to the mean?

Per-player goals-above-xG, then a hierarchical shrinkage model
(`glmer`, player random intercept, model-xG as a logit-scale offset). Low-volume
players are pulled toward zero.

![Shrinkage](`r fig("shrinkage.png")`){width=75%}

Top finishers by **shrunken** skill (with 95% intervals):

```{r leaderboard-skill}
head(rd("finishing_leaderboard.rds"), 10)
```

Split-half persistence test (odd/even shots; permutation null):

```{r persistence}
p <- rd("finishing_persistence.rds")
p
```

```{r verdict, results='asis'}
cat(sprintf(
  "**Verdict:** across %d players, split-half correlation of finishing over-performance is r = %.3f (permutation p = %.3f). %s\n",
  p$n_players, p$split_half_r, p$perm_p,
  if (p$perm_p < 0.05) "Some signal persists, but most raw over-performance is noise that shrinks heavily." else "We cannot reject that finishing over-performance in this sample is mostly noise."))
```

## Limitations

- **Selection bias**: open data skews toward elite competitions; finishing
  estimates may not transfer to lower levels.
- **No tracking data**: freeze frames are a snapshot at shot moment, not full
  positioning; defender/GK features are approximations.
- **Sample size**: the finishing-skill verdict is bounded by shots-per-player;
  the shrinkage and persistence results are about *this* sample.
- `statsbomb_xg` is a black box we benchmark against, not ground truth.

::: {.callout-note}
StatsBomb data is free for non-commercial use. Attribution: data provided by
StatsBomb (https://github.com/statsbomb/open-data).
:::
